title: crio-umount.conf and seccomp.json are present in both cri-o and libpod

comments.body:
We can drop the umount config here, it's not used elsewhere.

Seccomp should be moved out of both repositories and sourced from somewhere common - it's not specific to any of our tools. I think there's a containers-seccomp repo we created for this?
ETCDIR_LIBPOD is set to /etc/crio in the makefile, apparently, which seems very wrong
@mheon can't seem to find the containers-seccomp repo, do you have a link for that?

It lives here: https://github.com/seccomp/containers-golang

Unfortunately, I think my memory was faulty - it doesn't seem to include the defaults

@mheon I notice the doc mentions it generates a `default.json`. It's intended to be the same as our `seccomp.json`, yes? Also, not sure what defaults you mean, but is that something you can get included upstream maybe today? I guess I can create another package for containers-seccomp for ubuntu.
